procedural-formatter.posture: source is a script for the formatter; tags are imperative; static analysis undecidable in general

generic-markup.posture: source describes structure; rendering delegated to renderer parameterized by document type and target medium

nodes.label: Tunnicliffe (GCA, 1967)
nodes.contribution: Separation of content from format thesis

nodes.label: CTSS RUNOFF
nodes.contribution: First procedural text-formatter on a time-sharing system

nodes.label: SCRIPT (CP-67/CMS)
nodes.contribution: Procedural text-formatter; later co-deployed with GML in IBM DCF

nodes.label: SCRIPT/VS · DCF
nodes.contribution: IBM Document Composition Facility; GML+SCRIPT co-deployment

nodes.label: troff / nroff
nodes.contribution: Unix typesetter family; ancestor of groff and modern Unix manpages

nodes.label: GenCode (GCA)
nodes.contribution: Standardized generic publishing tags; merged into SGML standardization

nodes.label: GML (Generalized Markup Language)
nodes.contribution: First implemented generic-markup language; coined 'markup language' (1970)

nodes.label: SCRIBE
nodes.contribution: Parallel re-discovery of generic markup at CMU; doc/database split influenced LaTeX

nodes.label: SGML (ISO 8879:1986)
nodes.contribution: ISO standard generalized markup; DTD; SGML Declaration; entities; marked sections

nodes.label: DocBook
nodes.contribution: Technical-documentation DTD; later XML

nodes.label: TEI (Text Encoding Initiative)
nodes.contribution: Humanities-text encoding standard

nodes.label: TeX
nodes.contribution: Procedural typesetting system optimized for mathematics; Turing-complete macro language; boxes-and-glue model

nodes.label: METAFONT
nodes.contribution: Font-design language paired with TeX; Computer Modern font family

nodes.label: LaTeX
nodes.contribution: Generic-markup macro layer over TeX; documentclass model

nodes.label: ConTeXt
nodes.contribution: Alternative TeX macro package focused on flexible page layout

nodes.label: HTML
nodes.contribution: SGML application for hypertext on the Web

nodes.label: HTML 2.0 (RFC 1866)
nodes.contribution: First IETF-standardized HTML; first formal SGML DTD for HTML

nodes.label: HTML 4.01
nodes.contribution: Three DTDs (Strict, Transitional, Frameset); CSS dependency; deprecation of presentational tags

nodes.label: XML 1.0
nodes.contribution: Deliberate SGML simplification; one-page-parser discipline

nodes.label: XHTML 1.0/1.1
nodes.contribution: HTML 4 reformulated as XML 1.0 application

nodes.label: XHTML 2.0
nodes.contribution: From-scratch redesign; charter expired without Recommendation

nodes.label: HTML5 (Living Standard)
nodes.contribution: Specifies the lenient parser normatively; living standard

nodes.label: MathML 1.0
nodes.contribution: First XML application recommended by W3C; presentation/content/parallel split

nodes.label: MathML 4 / Core
nodes.contribution: Restricted to browser-implementable subset; aligned with HTML Living Standard

nodes.label: SVG 1.0
nodes.contribution: Web-native vector graphics; treated in depth in T2

nodes.label: XSLT 1.0 / XPath 1.0
nodes.contribution: Declarative XML transformation; XPath as query layer

nodes.label: XSD · Relax NG
nodes.contribution: Schema languages for XML beyond DTD

nodes.label: Markdown
nodes.contribution: Lightweight authoring surface that transcompiles to HTML

nodes.label: CommonMark
nodes.contribution: Unambiguous Markdown specification; reference implementations in C/JS/Haskell

nodes.label: reStructuredText
nodes.contribution: Lightweight markup with directive/role extension mechanism

nodes.label: AsciiDoc
nodes.contribution: Lightweight authoring surface for DocBook

nodes.label: Org-mode
nodes.contribution: Outline + executable-document substrate; ancestor of Jupyter pattern

nodes.label: Pandoc
nodes.contribution: Universal AST bridge; ~40 input formats x ~60 output formats; filter system

nodes.label: KaTeX (Appendix G)
nodes.contribution: Direct in-browser implementation of TeX Appendix G math layout

nodes.label: MathJax
nodes.contribution: Browser TeX renderer with internal MathML pipeline

nodes.label: LaTeXML
nodes.contribution: Full-LaTeX-to-XML; substrate of arXiv HTML rendering

nodes.label: Typst
nodes.contribution: Modern procedural-formatter with analyzable macro layer; Rust; instant preview

nodes.label: PGML
nodes.contribution: W3C Note; absorbed into SVG

nodes.label: VML
nodes.contribution: W3C Note; absorbed into SVG; survived in IE-specific code paths

nodes.label: XSL-FO
nodes.contribution: XML-to-print formatting-objects; FO Working Group closed 2013

nodes.label: SOAP / WSDL
nodes.contribution: XML messaging; superseded by JSON+REST/gRPC

nodes.label: DSSSL (ISO/IEC 10179:1996)
nodes.contribution: Scheme-based SGML stylesheets; superseded by XSLT

crossBranchEdges.evidence: Lamport drew on SCRIBE while at SRI; documented in doc 02 §5
TeX-source is the dominant authoring substrate; KaTeX, MathJax, Temml, LaTeXML render TeX into MathML; doc 05 §6
Markdown's design target is HTML output
Pandoc reads/writes LaTeX as one of ~40 formats
Pandoc reads/writes HTML as one of ~40 formats
AsciiDoc internal representation is DocBook XML

invariants.name: Renderer-independent tag identity
invariants.summary: Every successful descendant encodes structure in renderer-independent named tokens

invariants.name: Permissive authoring surface
invariants.summary: Successful descendants ship author surfaces more permissive than their data models

invariants.name: Tree data model
invariants.summary: Every member exposes the same rooted, ordered, attributed tree data model

invariants.name: Schemas migrate to tooling
invariants.summary: Schemas remain socially required but migrate from spec to toolchain over time

invariants.name: Parser before adoption
invariants.summary: Every successful member ships a usable parser before achieving adoption
